target.name: Sila Nanotechnologies
target.founder: Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, Gene Berdichevsky

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sila Nanotechnologies is a late-stage private company in the Climate & Energy sector, currently valued at $3.3B post-money following $375M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis relies on a peer set of 10 same-sector comparables spanning similar valuation tiers, including eight private companies (TAE Technologies at $6.0B, Redwood Materials at $6.0B, Helion at $5.4B, Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B) and one public comparable, QuantumScape (LNZA), also valued at $5.0B. The peer range spans $700M to $6.0B.

Two methods were applied to triangulate fair value. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.2x multiplier typical for Late Stage progression, yielding $4.0B from the $3.3B last post-money valuation. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comp set produced $4.2B, with a range floor of $700M and ceiling of $6.0B. The two methods converge closely, supporting a median implied valuation of $4.1B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3.4B to $4.9B, with a central point near $4.1B. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity within the peer set—stage diversity (Series B through public) and varied technology focuses within energy—and reliance on only two valuation methods. The current $3.3B valuation sits modestly below the derived range, suggesting potential upside in a follow-on round.
